DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-14-2021 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MERCHANT EXCHANGE PRODUCTIONS, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, NO. 1-10, AND REQUESTING MONETARY, ISSUE, AND EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND OR PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION GRANTED. DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO DISCOVER PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTION WHETHER THERE WAS A CUSTOM AND PRACTICE IN THE "BANQUET" INDUSTRY SPECIFICALLY. PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES WHICH REFER TO THE "FOOD AND BEVERAGE INDUSTRY" DOES NOT ANSWER THE QUESTION, NOR DOES THE REFERENCE TO DOCUMENTS. WHETHER THE "BANQUET INDUSTRY" IS SYNONYMOUS WITH THE "FOOD AND BEVERAGE INDUSTRY" IS SOMETHING THAT WILL BE RESOLVED ON DISPOSITIVE MOTIONS OR AT TRIAL PURSUANT TO THE COURT OF APPEAL DECISION IN THIS CASE. FOR PURPOSES OF DISCOVERY, BOTH SIDES ARE ENTITLED TO DISCOVER THE OTHER SIDE'S CONTENTIONS ON DISTINCTIONS BETWEEN THE "BANQUET" AND "FOOD AND BEVERAGE" INDUSTRIES. SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO BE PROVIDED BY JANUARY 29, 2021. SANCTIONS DENIED. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT DAVIDIDAHO14@GMAIL.COM. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, No. 1-10, And Requesting Monetary, Issue, And Evidentiary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Or Plaintiff?S Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SHANNON LISS-RIORDAN, Esq. of Lichten & Liss-Riordan, P.C. (Tel: 617-994-5800) for Plaintiff Lauren O'Grady, on behalf of herself and all others similarly situated;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MERCHANT EXCHANGE PRODUCTIONS, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, No. 1-10, And Requesting Monetary, Issue, And Evidentiary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Or Plaintiff's Counsel.

The Court determines that plaintiff did not properly contest the Court's tentative ruling and inform the opposing side of plaintiff's intent to appear today. Defendant is not present at today's hearing.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion granted. Defendant is entitled to discover Plaintiff's contention whether there was a custom and practice in the "banquet" industry specifically. Plaintiff's responses which refer to the "food and beverage industry" does not answer the question, nor does the reference to documents. Whether the "banquet industry" is synonymous with the "food and beverage industry" is something that will be resolved on dispositive motions or at trial pursuant to the Court of Appeal decision in this case. For purposes of discovery, both sides are entitled to discover the other side's contentions on distinctions between the "banquet" and "food and beverage" industries. Supplemental responses to be provided by January 29, 2021. Sanctions denied.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Feb-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF LAUREN O'GRADY'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE, the application to admit Ms. Liss-Riordan pro hac vice is denied. The application was filed on insufficient notice in violation of CCP 1005. Even if sufficient notice was given, the application would be denied since papers filed in Taranto v. Washio No. 546584 in this court establish that Ms. Liss-Riordan is ineligible for pro hac vice admission because she is regularly engaged in substantial business in the State of California.
